trans-unit.source: Working with Geodata
trans-unit.target: Working with Geodata

trans-unit.source: The second module develops a deeper understanding of geodata, layers and projections. These three concepts lie at the core of geoinformation systems and you will need a solid understanding in order to develop further GIS-skills. The module starts by explaining geodata, introducing the layer concept, and how to import geodata into QGIS. The second chapter will introduce the topic of projections and Coordinate Reference Systems. Displaying the world on a flat surface is not easy and depending on your use case, you will need to select appropriate projections. The third chapter goes over the management of geodata. Keeping your projects, datasets, and files organised is essential to produce the correct results, save you a lot of time, and ensure the reproducibility of your analyses. The fourth chapter takes a closer look at the attribute table and how to select data entries (so called geofeatures). The next chapter will introduce you to the art of finding data on the web, as well as introduce you to some databases where you can start looking for data. Finally, the last chapter will introduce basemaps, a small but very useful feature when looking at geodata or creating maps. Basemaps let you display a background map (for example, satellite imagery or topographic maps) without having all the different layers of geodata necessary for such a map on your computer.
trans-unit.target: The second module develops a deeper understanding of geodata, layers and projections. These three concepts lie at the core of geoinformation systems and you will need a solid understanding in order to develop further GIS-skills. The module starts by explaining geodata, introducing the layer concept, and how to import geodata into QGIS. The second chapter will introduce the topic of projections and Coordinate Reference Systems. Displaying the world on a flat surface is not easy and depending on your use case, you will need to select appropriate projections. The third chapter goes over the management of geodata. Keeping your projects, datasets, and files organised is essential to produce the correct results, save you a lot of time, and ensure the reproducibility of your analyses. The fourth chapter takes a closer look at the attribute table and how to select data entries (so called geofeatures). The next chapter will introduce you to the art of finding data on the web, as well as introduce you to some databases where you can start looking for data. Finally, the last chapter will introduce basemaps, a small but very useful feature when looking at geodata or creating maps. Basemaps let you display a background map (for example, satellite imagery or topographic maps) without having all the different layers of geodata necessary for such a map on your computer.

trans-unit.source: :::{list-table} Module 1 content :header-rows: 1 :widths: 150 600
trans-unit.target: :::{list-table} Module 1 content :header-rows: 1 :widths: 150 600

trans-unit.source: __Chapter__
trans-unit.target: __Chapter__

trans-unit.source: __Competences & Objectives__
trans-unit.target: __Competences & Objectives__

trans-unit.source: __[Chapter 1: Introduction to geodata and layer](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_geodata_concept.md)__
trans-unit.target: __[Chapter 1: Introduction to geodata and layer](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_geodata_concept.md)__

trans-unit.source: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__
trans-unit.target: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__

trans-unit.source: know the difference between vector and raster data,
trans-unit.target: know the difference between vector and raster data,

trans-unit.source: know different types of data formats,
trans-unit.target: know different types of data formats,

trans-unit.source: understand the layer concept,
trans-unit.target: understand the layer concept,

trans-unit.source: know how to import different formats of vector data into QGIS.
trans-unit.target: know how to import different formats of vector data into QGIS.

trans-unit.source: __[Chapter 2: Projections](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_projections.md)__
trans-unit.target: __[Chapter 2: Projections](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_projections.md)__

trans-unit.source: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__
trans-unit.target: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__

trans-unit.source: understand why it is difficult to portray the world on a flat surface,
trans-unit.target: understand why it is difficult to portray the world on a flat surface,

trans-unit.source: know what a coordinate reference system (CRS) is,
trans-unit.target: know what a coordinate reference system (CRS) is,

trans-unit.source: understand the difference between geographic and metric CRS,
trans-unit.target: understand the difference between geographic and metric CRS,

trans-unit.source: know how to choose an appropriate CRS,
trans-unit.target: know how to choose an appropriate CRS,

trans-unit.source: know how to reproject vector layers and how to change the QGIS-project CRS.
trans-unit.target: know how to reproject vector layers and how to change the QGIS-project CRS.

trans-unit.source: __[Chapter 3: Geodata Management](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_geodata_management.md)__
trans-unit.target: __[Chapter 3: Geodata Management](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_geodata_management.md)__

trans-unit.source: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__
trans-unit.target: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__

trans-unit.source: know how QGIS stores projects and data,
trans-unit.target: know how QGIS stores projects and data,

trans-unit.source: know how to set up a folder structure for QGIS-projects and geodata,
trans-unit.target: know how to set up a folder structure for QGIS-projects and geodata,

trans-unit.source: know good practices to name geodata files.
trans-unit.target: know good practices to name geodata files.

trans-unit.source: __[Chapter 4: The Attribute Table](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_attribute_table.md)__
trans-unit.target: __[Chapter 4: The Attribute Table](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_attribute_table.md)__

trans-unit.source: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__
trans-unit.target: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__

trans-unit.source: know how to open and view the attribute table of a dataset in QGIS,
trans-unit.target: know how to open and view the attribute table of a dataset in QGIS,

trans-unit.source: know how to sort the attribute table and zoom into specific features,
trans-unit.target: know how to sort the attribute table and zoom into specific features,

trans-unit.source: know how to select features.
trans-unit.target: know how to select features.

trans-unit.source: __[Chapter 5: Data Sources](/content/Module_2/en_data_sources.md)__
trans-unit.target: __[Chapter 5: Data Sources](/content/Module_2/en_data_sources.md)__

trans-unit.source: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__
trans-unit.target: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__

trans-unit.source: know what to consider when searching for geodata,
trans-unit.target: know what to consider when searching for geodata,

trans-unit.source: know a few websites to find specific data,
trans-unit.target: know a few websites to find specific data,

trans-unit.source: know the collaborative mapping project OpenStreetMap,
trans-unit.target: know the collaborative mapping project OpenStreetMap,

trans-unit.source: know how to export OpenStreetMap data.
trans-unit.target: know how to export OpenStreetMap data.

trans-unit.source: __[Chapter 6: Basemaps](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_basemap.md)__
trans-unit.target: __[Chapter 6: Basemaps](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_basemap.md)__

trans-unit.source: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__
trans-unit.target: __At the end of this chapter, you should:__

trans-unit.source: know how to add basemaps to your QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: know how to add basemaps to your QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __[Exercises for Module 2](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_module_2_exercises.md)__
trans-unit.target: __[Exercises for Module 2](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_module_2_exercises.md)__

trans-unit.source: All the exercises for module 5 are listed here.
trans-unit.target: All the exercises for module 5 are listed here.
